title: 发明第一个字母
subtitle: 声音可以用符号来记录吗？
learningGoal: 理解字母是声音的编码符号——'发明' /m/ 对应的字母 m
learningGoalEn: Understand that letters are encoding symbols for sounds — 'invent' the letter m for /m/

hints.text: 你已经有了 /m/ 这个声音。如果能用一个符号来'写'这个声音，就不用每次都发出声来了。把音素水晶拖到空白石板上试试！
hints.textEn: You have the /m/ sound. If a symbol could 'write' this sound, you wouldn't need to vocalize it. Drag the phoneme crystal onto the blank tablet!

hints.text: 石板和声音水晶之间有某种神秘的联系……当声音被'注入'到石板上，石板就会自动刻出一个代表这个声音的符号！
hints.textEn: There's a mysterious connection between the tablet and crystal... when sound is 'poured' into the tablet, it carves a symbol representing that sound!

failureModes.descriptionEn: Has not dragged crystal to tablet

introText: 你掌握了声音，也学会了合成。但每次都需要发声或播放录音，太麻烦了！古代文明的文字大厅里有一些空白石板——传说它们能'吸收'声音并产生对应的符号。把 /m/ 音素水晶放到石板上，看看会发生什么？
introTextEn: You've mastered sounds and blending. But producing sounds every time is tedious! The ancient Scribing Hall has blank tablets — legends say they 'absorb' sounds and produce corresponding symbols. Place the /m/ crystal onto a tablet — what will happen?
victoryText: 石板发光了！一个从未见过的符号——'m'——被刻在了石板上。你刚刚'发明'了人类历史上第一个字母！这个符号读作 /m/，它让声音变得可以被'看见'。这就是文字的起源——声音的视觉编码。
victoryTextEn: The tablet glowed! An unknown symbol — 'm' — carved itself into the stone. You just 'invented' the first letter in history! This symbol is read as /m/, letting sound become 'visible.' This is the origin of writing — visual encoding of sound.
